consumes.artifact: specs/<spec-id>/ (target audit spec, identified by the skill argument)

produces.artifact: Main-session terminal output (report markdown + AskUserQuestion flows + Decisions table)

requirement.title: User-invoked skill with spec-id argument
requirement.description: The skill MUST be invocable via `/arc-auditing-spec <spec-id>` and MUST fail cleanly when the target spec-id does not exist under `specs/`. No pipeline stage — brainstorming, refining, or planning — may be modified to auto-invoke this skill.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a user runs `/arc-auditing-spec <id>` and `specs/<id>/` exists, When the skill starts, Then it MUST proceed to Phase 1 (fan-out) without error and without writing any file to disk at this point. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Invocation

Given a user runs `/arc-auditing-spec <id>` and `specs/<id>/` does NOT exist, When the skill starts, Then it MUST fail with a terminal message that lists the currently available spec-ids under `specs/`, MUST NOT spawn any sub-agent, and MUST NOT write any file. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Invocation

Given the arc-auditing-spec skill is installed, When arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, or arc-planning completes its Stage Completion, Then none of those skills' bodies MUST contain a direct or indirect invocation of `/arc-auditing-spec`. The only trigger path is the user typing the command. design.md §Scope — Out of scope (Pipeline auto-invocation)

requirement.title: Hard read-only boundaries enforced via tool grants
requirement.description: The skill body and all three sub-agents MUST NOT modify any file or execute any mutating git or filesystem action. Enforcement MUST be via restricted tool grants in the agent definitions themselves, not via prose instructions inside system prompts.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given any invocation of the skill, When the skill body and its sub-agents execute, Then they MUST NOT modify any file under `specs/`, `docs/`, `scripts/`, `skills/`, `agents/`, `hooks/`, or `templates/`. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Hard boundaries

Given any invocation of the skill, When the sub-agents execute, Then they MUST NOT perform git commit, git branch creation, git worktree creation, or any file-delete operation. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Hard boundaries

Given each sub-agent definition file, When its tool allowlist is inspected, Then the allowlist MUST include only read tools (Read / Grep / Glob) and MUST NOT include Edit, Write, or any state-mutating tool — such that the read-only constraint is enforced by the tool grant rather than relying on prompt instructions. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Hard boundaries

requirement.title: Implementation produced via arc-writing-skills
requirement.description: The SKILL.md body (its phase content), the three sub-agent system prompts, and eval scenarios validating audit correctness MUST all be produced through arc-writing-skills' TDD + eval-driven flow during implementation. This spec specifies observable behavior and interface contracts only, not the literal markdown content of those artifacts.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given this spec reaches the implementation epic, When deliverable markdown files (SKILL.md, agent files, eval scenarios) are produced, Then they MUST go through arc-writing-skills' RED / GREEN / REFACTOR cycle with recorded baseline (without-skill) and validated (with-skill) behaviors. design.md §Architecture — Implementation delegation

Given the skill is ready to ship, When the eval suite is inspected, Then it MUST contain at least one eval scenario exercising each of the three audit axes (cross-artifact-alignment, internal-consistency, state-transition-integrity). design.md §Architecture — Implementation delegation
